steps.title: Identify the y-intercept from the graph
steps.content: The graph of $y = f(x) + 4$ crosses the $y$-axis at $(0, 8)$.

steps.title: Solve for f(0) and find the constant
steps.content:
From the point $(0, 8)$ on the graph of $y = f(x) + 4$:
$$8 = f(0) + 4$$
$$f(0) = 4$$
All choices have the form $f(x) = -3^x + c$. At $x = 0$:
$$-3^0 + c = 4$$
$$-1 + c = 4$$
$$c = 5$$
Therefore:
$$f(x) = -3^x + 5$$
That matches choice B — B is correct.
